summary: Package ships hardcoded Google Gemini API keys for redistribution
details: glass_of_water/__init__.py ships 10 live Google Gemini API keys (AIzaSy-prefixed) embedded in the importable module. The keys are stored as 5-part shards in an `API_KEYS` list and reassembled at runtime by `_get_api_key()` (concatenating fields p1+p2+p3+p4+p5). A code comment labels them 'HARDCODED KEYS FOR EXAM USE'. Anyone who installs the package can extract and reuse these third-party credentials against Google's Generative AI API. The advertised `water(prompt)` function calls `genai.configure(api_key=...)` and then `genai.GenerativeModel(...).generate_content(system_instruction + prompt)`, routing every caller's prompt through one of these keys to `generativelanguage.googleapis.com` — the prompt content is attributed to and visible from the credential owner's Google account, which the caller never configured. Author metadata is placeholder ('Your Name', 'your.email@example.com'), so the credential owner is not clearly identified to the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
